parsed_verdict.reasoning: The rule set has 11 top-level rules (Stage 1 and Stage 2 are parallel formulations of the same 11 rules, not two separate sets), which is within the N9 parsimony budget. No two rules paraphrase the same claim — each addresses a distinct phenomenon (natural place, weight-dependent fall, medium resistance, shape, wind, floating, dragged carts, dragging strain, projectile, flame, heavens). Every rule traces to specific observations cited in the text, and the impetus-fading account of the arrow is a permitted Aristotelian elaboration. Stage 2 contains multiple cross-rule references (e.g., 'speed is set by Rules 2–4', 'combined with Rule 2', 'Rule 4 takes over', 'downward tendency (Rule 1)'), satisfying N12 hierarchy.
